title: Settling in at White Spar Campground
summary:
Abby made a pit stop in Prescott on her way to meet up with us and loved it so much that she wanted to head back before making her way back to Missoula. Since we've been living pretty day to day, we had nothing on our agenda and decided to join. We stayed at a very nice little campground about 5 minutes from downtown Prescott. It ended up being such a nice spot that we both extended our reservation two different times! The camp hosts were very friendly even though we made them change out the reservation sign three times.

On Saturday Abby and I hiked the loop around Watson Lake while Colin ran. It's been nice to have a hiking buddy since Colin and I often split up on hikes so he can do a longer run. Maya and Wynn had lots of fun scrambling over rocks and trying to drink the dirty lake water. We were all starving after the outing, so we headed into town and ate at the Barley Hound (highly recommend!). They had a nice outdoor patio, amazing food, including duck fat fries, beet salad, duck burger, and chili wontons, and they even treated the dogs to a big plate of rice and steak pieces.

We spent the next few days going on little hikes after camp, playing games, cooking group meals, running van errands like laundry, showers and groceries and sitting around the campfire at night. Having Abby here to hang out with and the added hour of daylight has coaxed our bedtime to be slightly later than our usual 7:30pm. The campground was also attached to a very fun bike park, called Bean Peaks, that Colin and I got to ride one afternoon. The trails were all named after different beans (cool beans, limey beans, jelly beans, etc) and they were very flowy compared to what we are used to in Missoula.

We finally decided it was time to head out and split ways (we'll miss you, Abby!) and headed off in the direction of Payson after a shower at Planet Fitness and grocery resupply at Trader Joe's.

-Helen

photos.caption: Our yummy spread of food from The Barley Hound.
The rissoto dinner that Abby cooked for us on our last night in Prescott.
Colin excited about the mountain bike trails at Bean Peaks bike park.
A selfie after finishing a lap at Bean Peaks.
A selfie with Abby before we headed separate ways.
